body:
There are 185 days until the midterm general elections. Primaries have already started. State legislative sessions are in full swing, with more right around the corner, and suppressive bills are on the rise again.
What does this all mean? Our work is going full speed right now , not waiting for the summer. The foundation we lay right now is what sets the stage for November's elections.
RUSH $35 or more right now and help make every vote count and every voice heard in our elections nationwide >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
The Atlantic: Donald Trump’s Plan to Subvert the Midterms Is Already Under Way [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=25]
Team, that headline from The Atlantic says it all.
Donald Trump already promised as part of his 2024 campaign that “ we’ll have it fixed so good, you’re not gonna have to vote .” Watching his actions after he lost in 2020 -- lies, denial, rallies, all leading to January 6th -- it's not hard to imagine what he's got in mind for November.
Our states run our elections. But that doesn't mean that MAGA-aligned politicians won't follow his illegal orders to interfere. It doesn't stop him from using ICE agents to intimidate at the polls or threaten eligible voters. And it doesn't stop his Department of Justice from trying to interfere through the courts, especially when more than half of the suppressive lawsuits brought to court this year were brought by the very federal department that's supposed to protect our rights.
None of that takes into account the highly partisan efforts for mid-decade redistricting as well. It's clear that the threats to the next election aren't going to stop. That's where we come in.
We must inform voters, defend our elections, and make sure every eligible vote counts. Chip in with $35 or whatever you can today to fuel our voting rights work for 2026 and beyond >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=050226_2&amount=50]

Our democracy, our elections, and our fundamental right to vote have been under attack for more than a decade. Our electoral system is reaching a breaking point, and it's hard to predict exactly where the next set of electoral shenanigans will come from.
Are we destined to watch our elections break down? Absolutely not. We still have the time and the people to win. What we need most is the resources to carry on this fight for our democracy and the work to make every voice heard in our elections.
Stopping their attempts to subvert the midterms will require courage and integrity across the board. Most of all, it will depend on individual Americans like us to stand up for our rights and demand that our votes are counted.
This is a fight we can and will win. Stand with us to protect the right to vote by pitching in with $35 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=050226_2&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050226_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=050226_2&amount=20]

# Sunday, May 3, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1986, Willie Shoemaker, 54, guided 17-1 long shot Ferdinand to a Kentucky Derby win, becoming the race's oldest winning jockey and redeeming his infamous 1957 finish-line misjudgment. ## Court Blocks Abortion Pill Access by Mail, Telehealth
A federal appeals court ruled unanimously on Friday to [block the ability of doctors](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDHS2yAQRk-DOnnWixD6C4o06pw2pQbBYm28Egwidnz7jJMjpP6-eW_m_bgtN8_Cx_2738m1lJeDXqdQa1SXlsuvk34XqkxHoLNweAitXhYEHHswPeglepb3MnTRJSIk3ZG7WmsnwMkM3eamL-PXaRhNTDRGj1ZHCGDHZFYKtGLH7kMDA_oKYDVeNEDUmNJqg4WvYVUDUOSW6-Vn5qNtlOSdn1QvIe-duK21cir9TeGscG4bbSzy2RTOJQuHt8J5Iy9tC76SwtlMI1y16XdOVCqfLR_UNxL69-pLpTNULo3z0a-Sw4OiwrmrLpYXi_A5WjXAfff8V9S1_yr3dPgnAAD___eGhFA) to prescribe the abortion pill mifepristone through telehealth and mail delivery. The court issued a temporary nationwide injunction that reinstates a requirement that mifepristone must be prescribed and dispensed in person. The Food and Drug Administration permanently lifted the in-person requirement in 2023 after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade in 2022. Telehealth prescriptions have been [key to maintaining abortion access](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL-O4yAYxJ_G7rA-g21yBcU16XLtlRZ_PmIunwEBiS9vv_KutC-w5einGc3M39t604FCvP_RO6rm0xrxqIStYVlbys-K_zOWgNFizcE-CI2mlQNfGMwMxOp0oPc69U55RI6iRzVKKS_AL_PUb2p0KEfuEdHBAmiNwGXmi1hGPUvvXB_UmQYziBFACj4IACe490ZaCb8m002ALrRUhn8pxLahp3d6YRls2ntSW2u5duJ3x68dvx7HMURjzxUn7_h1Q01tO1HaMVb2rXXOqKkym56lMUPJPirTJpUWUmQ5EDFtLdbKGhJ-2diuA7FioxaTGKdLX5TLRyAKdZHdBPeTfxZrP3rzpfhHAAAA__8s1Iwf) in states that outlawed or restricted the practice. Surveys have found that the majority of abortions in the U.S. are [provided via pills](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFygzAQRL9GdGIOCZBdqEhD57QpGSFO9sUCMdLZ2H-fIfmENNvszpud93UZL44irddPt6DlkMYV9xKRGfPIaXsUfG2YCVePZSN_jzi5OCpQvYROgh5nR_E9ttVsA6JCXaFtjDEnUKeurW52OjemhdmHrjVdCOi0813fnJ0OTe8MVGQPGnSgGwCjVa0BZq1CmIw3cG4n0QLOxCnX34lWvmGI7_TEXPu0VNHemLci9IdQg1DDvu91SK8G-Hn0Qg0HXagBuiMaoQafHpllxsKZPBfpppSZ0iqd91iKdD6nUuSjyOktp5j8ndarXP48yYUCbpkKpxWFGqps522nGKn0RrRwPXa_z_hfOp9W_QQAAP__yLGKbA) and that about 1 in 4 abortions nationally are prescribed via telehealth.

**Whatever happened to Tab diet soda?** Tab may be the only soft drink that managed to invent a category, lose it to its own successor, and still inspire petitions for its return decades later. Introduced by Coca-Cola in 1963, Tab was the company's first diet drink, arriving as calorie counting began to enter the American mainstream. Early ads targeted women, tying the drink, with its [iconic pink can](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOozAQBOCnMV3QZm0wFC7S0OWqk65Exl6TvRhjGV9yeftf_K_wlyN9mpHmz32-W46c1l92I1PDPid6H5FqpTLXPf876H-mwpQcHZndM9Ji44yA_QW6C8jZW46fWTXeBCIk2ZC5aq0HwKFTzcNA1wV1VQP1wS4u2EV1yvejptHBuLihYXO2QQfyCqAlthLASwxh0U7DqBahgDzXvbR_d071QSF-9heV1u1bE82j1nwIeRM4CZwotW9-cibPtt3LKnA6s8Dpt11mgYMvnJ4CR4FS4LSdTuA0cSQhb6dxNrU5rU0xPr85Rj56LRSsm-X4PVl_9NPL4FcAAAD__531ezE), to slimness and control. [Here's one of their commercials](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwjAUxfGnsTeiy70OhsFDpYpOsFbqYvnjhpg6cZQYUt6-Sl-h6znSb_h_XuzFpZzG29UNbGpX7MjrkrlWnm0t02Phn4nnxGPgZUrhO7N32SLgYQftDshGl_LLKhlNx4xMks1ea30EPLZK9ubkwJOifauOBAoZOXgfo6KTPkQMrUxm06AF2gNowoYAImHXeR00nJQXCjimWubmXtJYe-7yqzx5bkIZZDZ9rdMi6E3gWeB5XdfmVR714Xn7t8XV0As6PwW980fI169-uFOQs4nTmnJOy0ELBbfBpfxH1n91eBr8DQAA__8VlXEx), featuring the catchy Tab jingle. The strategy worked. By the 1970s, Tab was the dominant diet soda in the United States and helped define an entirely new category. Its demise began with the success of a different kind. In 1982, Coca-Cola launched Diet Coke, a sleeker product backed by the company's flagship brand. Consumers migrated quickly.

Tab, once the innovator, became the legacy option. Health scares around saccharin and recipe tweaks did not help. By the 1990s, Tab had faded from the mainstream, kept alive largely out of habit and loyalty. That loyalty proved unusually durable. Tab developed a devoted following of drinkers who refused to switch, often describing the taste as irreplaceable. When rumors of discontinuation surfaced, fans stockpiled cases and traded tips on where to find remaining cans. Coca-Cola finally ended production in 2020, citing a need to streamline its portfolio. The reaction was immediate. [Petitions circulated, advocacy groups formed, and diehards pushed for a comeback](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO4yAQQL_G7iYaA147BcU16fbaK60xHuy5xYCAJJu_P-V-YesnPT29P5_LJ0mQuP-mk23zaYn8rIFb47K0lO-VvzMX4ei4ZnFfgVcKi0L1ATgC6mUjCa_F9Jv1zIp1z3aYpmlGNY-mPyzjcDXzOGo3z4RMRMqv7rqSIj8rbXqxbxuOqAfESauLRty08n6d3IRXs3YGeZOWyuVvktgO9uGVHlwuLp19sEdruXb6V6dunbplTjnwG3Xq5ilWcHRmkj2CTwUIGq1Q00ZQuN1LBIobtIOlAGdxwN6n0iqc9IKV4ZnKl8Qd-NuFe5UHwzDMWptx6Ivd8lNCkPoxdQb3kyT8T2o_-viw6l8AAAD__0TqiQI). Tab's story is not just about a product that lost market share: It's about what happens when a brand outlives its moment but not its core appeal. _Were you a fan of Tab, and do you still miss it? Tab may be the only soft drink that managed to invent a category, lose it to its own successor, and still inspire petitions for its return decades later. Introduced by Coca-Cola in 1963, Tab was the company's first diet drink, arriving as calorie counting began to enter the American mainstream. Early ads targeted women, tying the drink, with its , to slimness and control. , featuring the catchy Tab jingle. The strategy worked. By the 1970s, Tab was the dominant diet soda in the United States and helped define an entirely new category. Its demise began with the success of a different kind. In 1982, Coca-Cola launched Diet Coke, a sleeker product backed by the company's flagship brand. Consumers migrated quickly. Tab, once the innovator, became the legacy option. Health scares around saccharin and recipe tweaks did not help. By the 1990s, Tab had faded from the mainstream, kept alive largely out of habit and loyalty. That loyalty proved unusually durable. Tab developed a devoted following of drinkers who refused to switch, often describing the taste as irreplaceable. When rumors of discontinuation surfaced, fans stockpiled cases and traded tips on where to find remaining cans. Coca-Cola finally ended production in 2020, citing a need to streamline its portfolio. The reaction was immediate. . Tab's story is not just about a product that lost market share: It's about what happens when a brand outlives its moment but not its core appeal.
